cee: noun. The name of the Latin script letter C / c.
cees: noun. plural of cee, the name of the letter C.
censer: noun. An ornamental container for burning incense, especially during religious ceremonies.
censor: noun. One of the two magistrates who originally administered the census of citizens, and by Classical times (between the 8th century B.C.E.
cere: noun. A fleshy, waxy area at the base of the upper beak in certain birds.
cero: noun. A large scombroid food fish (Scomberomorus regalis) found chiefly in the West Indies.
con: verb. To study or examine carefully, especially in order to gain knowledge of; to learn, or learn by heart.
cone: noun. A surface of revolution formed by rotating a segment of a line around another line that intersects the first line.
conserve: noun. Wilderness where human development is prohibited.
converse: verb. To talk; to engage in conversation.
core: noun. In general usage, an essential part of a thing surrounded by other essential things.
corn: noun. Any cereal plant (or its grain) that is the main crop or staple of a country or region.
corvee: noun. Unpaid labor required by a feudal lord.
cos: noun. Synonym of romaine lettuce, a long-leaved variety of lettuce.
cove: noun. A hollow in a rock; a cave or cavern.
encore: noun. A brief extra performance, done after the main performance is complete.
ens: noun. An entity or being; an existing thing, as opposed to a quality or attribute.
eon: noun. Eternity, the duration of the universe.
erne: noun. A sea eagle (Haliaeetus), especially the white-tailed eagle (Haliaeetus albicilla)
eros: noun. A winged figure of a child representing love or its power.
erose: adjective. Irregularly notched, eaten away, as though bitten.
ers: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of er
eve: noun. The day or night before, usually used for holidays, such as Christmas Eve.
nee: interjection. no, used to express no as a quantity, i.e. not any, like German kein /Dutch geen /French rien.
nerve: noun. A bundle of neurons with their connective tissue sheaths, blood vessels and lymphatics.
never: adverb. At no time; on no occasion; in no circumstance.
nor: conjunction. And... not (introducing a negative statement, without necessarily following one).
nose: noun. A protuberance on the face housing the nostrils, which are used to breathe or smell.
once: adverb. One and only one time.
one: noun. NATO & ICAO radiotelephony clear code (spelling-alphabet name) for the digit 1.
ons: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of on
orc: noun. Any of several large, ferocious sea creatures, now especially the killer whale.
ore: noun. Rock or other material that contains valuable or utilitarian materials; primarily a rock containing metals or gems for which it is
oven: noun. A chamber used for baking or heating.
ree: noun. Alternative form of rei (“Portuguese real”).
rev: verb. To increase the speed of a motor, or to operate at a higher speed.
roc: noun. An enormous mythical bird in Eastern legend.
rose: noun. A shrub of the genus Rosa, with red, pink, white or yellow flowers.
roves: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of rove
scene: noun. The location of an event that attracts attention.
scone: noun. A small, rich, pastry or quick bread, sometimes baked on a griddle.
score: noun. The total number of goals, points, runs, etc. earned by a participant in a game.
scorn: verb. To feel or display contempt or disdain for something or somebody; to despise.
scree: noun. Loose stony debris on a slope.
screen: noun. A physical divider intended to block an area from view, or provide shelter from something dangerous.
secern: verb. To separate or set apart (someone or something from other persons or things).
see: verb. To perceive or detect someone or something with the eyes, or as if by sight.
seer: noun. One who foretells the future; a clairvoyant, prophet, soothsayer or diviner.
ser: noun. An old Indian unit of weight, equal to 80 tolas, or one fortieth of a maund.
serve: noun. An act of putting the ball or shuttlecock in play in various games.
seven: noun. NATO & ICAO radiotelephony clear code (spelling-alphabet name) for the digit 7.
sneer: verb. To raise a corner of the upper lip slightly, especially in scorn.
snore: verb. To breathe during sleep with harsh, snorting noises caused by vibration of the soft palate.
soever: adverb. In any fashion, of any kind; used for emphasis after words such as how, what, which etc.
sone: noun. a subjective unit of loudness for an average listener equal to the loudness of a 1000-hertz sound that has an intensity 40 decibels
sore: adjective. Causing pain or discomfort; painfully sensitive.
sorn: verb. to impose upon another for food and lodging.
vee: noun. The name of the Latin script letter V / v.
veer: verb. To let out (a sail-line), to allow (a sheet) to run out.
venose: adjective. Having numerous or conspicuous veins; veiny.
verse: noun. A poetic form with regular meter and a fixed rhyme scheme.

voe: noun. A narrow sea inlet, particularly in the Shetland Islands, similar to a fjord.
ceo: noun. Alternative letter-case form of CEO.
cns: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Central Asmat.
coner: noun. A marijuana cigarette tapered to a conical shape.
cose: verb. To make oneself cosy; to be snug.
creen: verb. To complain; to whine; to moan.
cro: noun. Synonym of bro (“a male comrade or friend”).
crosne: noun. A vegetable, of species Stachys affinis; a Chinese artichoke.
crs: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Seychellois Creole.
eco: adjective. Clipping of ecological: environmentally friendly or sensitive.
esne: noun. A hireling of servile status; slave.
evoe: interjection. Alternative form of euoi (“Bacchic cry”).
nco: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Sibe.

reen: noun. A running waterway that links a ditch or stream to a river.
screeno: noun. A form of bingo played in American movie theaters during the Great Depression, with numbers displayed on the screen.
soree: noun. Obsolete form of sora (the bird).
